currentStep.openingLine: Let's turn absolute value back into distance before we touch the symbols.
currentStep.firstTutorQuestion: How far is this number from 0 on the number line?
currentStep.smallHint: Absolute value tells distance, so the answer itself is not negative.
currentStep.goodStoppingPoint: Learner explains absolute value as distance and uses that idea to compare values correctly.
watchFor: Learner keeps the negative sign when writing an absolute-value answer.
Learner forgets that zero has the smallest absolute value.
currentStep.exampleTutorMove: Point to -4 on a number line and ask for the distance to 0 before asking for |-4|.
currentStep.exampleLearnerResponse: The distance from -4 to 0 is 4, so |-4| is 4, not -4.
currentStep.title: Reframe Absolute Value as Distance
